query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "omniretrieval: unified retrieval across heterogeneous knowledge source" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the core mechanism of "Generative Retrieval" (the "Xive" component of the user's query) and analyzes the impact of document identifier design (linguistic vs. numeric) on retrieval performance, which is directly relevant to extending retrieval systems across heterogeneous or evolving knowledge sources. Although it focuses on dynamic corpora rather than the specific "OmniRetrieval" framework, it provides the necessary empirical baseline
